description:
A FinOps analyst wants to keep a very lean, reproducible backup of the raw cost-usage CSVs that currently live in:

    /home/user/finops/raw_cost_data/

  Your job is to:
    1. Create a compressed archive of the entire raw_cost_data directory.
       • Name the archive exactly:
            /home/user/finops/backups/cost-data-backup.tar.gz
       • If the parent directory “/home/user/finops/backups” does not exist, create it first.
    2. Produce a plain-text log file that summarises the backup operation.
       • Name the log file exactly:
            /home/user/finops/backups/backup_log.txt
       • Overwrite any existing file of that name.
       • The log must contain three lines and ONLY three lines, each terminating with a single newline character “\n”.  
         The format (including capitalisation, spacing, colons and commas) MUST be:

             Archive Name: cost-data-backup.tar.gz
             Original Size (bytes): <ORIGINAL_BYTES>
             Compressed Size (bytes): <COMPRESSED_BYTES>

         where:
           – <ORIGINAL_BYTES> is the total size, in bytes, of every regular file located
             inside /home/user/finops/raw_cost_data/ **before** the archival step.
           – <COMPRESSED_BYTES> is the size, in bytes, of /home/user/finops/backups/cost-data-backup.tar.gz
             **after** it has been created.

  Constraints & hints:
    • Only standard GNU/Linux user-land tools that are already present in a minimal Debian/Ubuntu image may be used.
    • You should not hard-code either size; calculate them programmatically.
    • No additional text or blank lines should appear in backup_log.txt.
    • A successful solution typically needs no more than 2-3 shell commands.

  The automated grader will check:
    • That /home/user/finops/backups/cost-data-backup.tar.gz exists and is a valid gzip archive.
    • That backup_log.txt exists, follows the exact three-line format shown above and contains accurate byte counts.
truth:
Initial on-disk state (exists before the agent starts):

    Directory: /home/user/finops/raw_cost_data/

      ├── january.csv
      └── february.csv

  Exact file contents (each line ends with “\n”):

    /home/user/finops/raw_cost_data/january.csv
        Date,Cost\n
        Compute,23.45\n
        Storage,12.00\n

    /home/user/finops/raw_cost_data/february.csv
        Date,Cost\n
        Compute,25.10\n
        Storage,11.50\n

  Byte counts:
    january.csv  = 38 bytes
    february.csv = 38 bytes
    TOTAL        = 76 bytes

  The test suite will:
    • Verify that the archive path is exactly /home/user/finops/backups/cost-data-backup.tar.gz
    • Verify that the log file path is /home/user/finops/backups/backup_log.txt
    • Read the log file and extract the two numeric fields.
    • Independently compute:
        – the current size of all regular files inside /home/user/finops/raw_cost_data/ (pre-archive)
        – the size in bytes of the archive itself (post-archive)
      and assert equality with the numbers written in the log.
    • Confirm there are precisely three lines in the log and that they match the required static prefixes.

  No files or directories other than those mentioned need to be present before the task begins.
